Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: In situ tuning of FCC–BCC dual phase and mechanical properties in multi-principal element alloys via laser-aided additive manufacturing

Composition_Info.Note: Pre-alloyed FCC-structured CoCrNi powder feedstock composition from Table 1; fabricated block composition measured via EDS

Process_Text.original: Laser-aided additive manufacturing (LAAM) using Kuka robotic system, Precitec laser head, Sulzer metco twin powder feeder, and IPG ytterbium fiber laser. 100% CoCrNi powder feed (0% CoCrNiAl0.6TiFe). Parameters: 1.3 mm beam diameter, 570 W laser power, 5.4 g/min total powder feed rate, 1200 mm/min scan velocity, ~0.5 mm layer thickness, 90° rotation between adjacent layers, 50% overlap rate, argon shielding (purity ≥99.999%).
Process_Text.simplified: LAAM from 100% FCC CoCrNi powder; 570 W, 1200 mm/min, 5.4 g/min powder feed

Process_Info.Equipment: Kuka robotic system, Precitec laser head, Sulzer metco twin powder feeder, IPG ytterbium fiber laser

Microstructure_Text.original: Predominantly FCC phase structure with lattice parameter 0.3282 nm. Round-shaped chromium oxide nanoparticles distributed sporadically in the matrix. No elemental segregation observed. Fine grains due to non-uniform solidification and high cooling rates in LAAM process.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC matrix with chromium oxide nanoparticles; no elemental segregation; fine grain structure

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; strain rate 0.6 mm/min
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: 25 mm × 6 mm × 4 mm coupon, wire cut EDM

Process_Text.original: Laser-aided additive manufacturing (LAAM) using Kuka robotic system, Precitec laser head, Sulzer metco twin powder feeder, and IPG ytterbium fiber laser. 90% CoCrNi powder + 10% CoCrNiAl0.6TiFe powder feed. Parameters: 1.3 mm beam diameter, 570 W laser power, 5.4 g/min total powder feed rate, 1200 mm/min scan velocity, ~0.5 mm layer thickness, 90° rotation between adjacent layers, 50% overlap rate, argon shielding (purity ≥99.999%).
Process_Text.simplified: LAAM from 90% FCC CoCrNi + 10% BCC CoCrNiAl0.6TiFe powder; 570 W, 1200 mm/min, 5.4 g/min powder feed

Process_Info.Equipment: Kuka robotic system, Precitec laser head, Sulzer metco twin powder feeder, IPG ytterbium fiber laser

Microstructure_Text.original: Predominantly FCC phase structure with expanded lattice parameter (0.3291 nm) due to solid solution effect of Al and Ti. No B2 phase precipitates. Aluminum oxide nanoparticles present. No elemental segregation observed; FCC and BCC powders fully mixed and melted during LAAM.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC matrix with lattice expansion; no B2 precipitates; aluminum oxide nanoparticles; no elemental segregation

Process_Text.original: Laser-aided additive manufacturing (LAAM) using Kuka robotic system, Precitec laser head, Sulzer metco twin powder feeder, and IPG ytterbium fiber laser. 80% CoCrNi powder + 20% CoCrNiAl0.6TiFe powder feed. Parameters: 1.3 mm beam diameter, 570 W laser power, 5.4 g/min total powder feed rate, 1200 mm/min scan velocity, ~0.5 mm layer thickness, 90° rotation between adjacent layers, 50% overlap rate, argon shielding (purity ≥99.999%).

Microstructure_Text.original: Predominantly FCC phase structure with lattice parameter 0.3297 nm. Numerous aluminum oxide nanoparticle clusters distributed in matrix. No elemental segregation; FCC and BCC powders fully mixed and melted. No B2 phase precipitates. Complex dislocation networks at dendritic network boundaries acting as strengthening elements.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC matrix with Al oxide nanoparticles; no B2 precipitates; dislocation strengthening at cell boundaries

Process_Text.original: Laser-aided additive manufacturing (LAAM) using Kuka robotic system, Precitec laser head, Sulzer metco twin powder feeder, and IPG ytterbium fiber laser. 70% CoCrNi powder + 30% CoCrNiAl0.6TiFe powder feed. Parameters: 1.3 mm beam diameter, 570 W laser power, 5.4 g/min total powder feed rate, 1200 mm/min scan velocity, ~0.5 mm layer thickness, 90° rotation between adjacent layers, 50% overlap rate, argon shielding (purity ≥99.999%).

Microstructure_Text.original: Predominantly FCC phase structure with lattice parameter 0.3303 nm. Nanoparticle clusters dispersed within matrix (similar to x=0.2). No elemental segregation; FCC and BCC powders fully mixed and melted. Dislocation-mediated hardening dominant with characteristic dislocation density of 2.5×10^15 m^-2. No B2 phase precipitation.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC matrix with Al oxide nanoparticles; dislocation density 2.5×10^15 m^-2; dislocation strengthening dominant; no B2 precipitates

Process_Text.original: Laser-aided additive manufacturing (LAAM) using Kuka robotic system, Precitec laser head, Sulzer metco twin powder feeder, and IPG ytterbium fiber laser. 60% CoCrNi powder + 40% CoCrNiAl0.6TiFe powder feed. Parameters: 1.3 mm beam diameter, 570 W laser power, 5.4 g/min total powder feed rate, 1200 mm/min scan velocity, ~0.5 mm layer thickness, 90° rotation between adjacent layers, 50% overlap rate, argon shielding (purity ≥99.999%).
Process_Text.simplified: LAAM from 60% FCC CoCrNi + 40% BCC CoCrNiAl0.6TiFe powder; 570 W, 1200 mm/min, 5.4 g/min powder feed; B2 phase precipitation begins

Process_Info.Equipment: Kuka robotic system, Precitec laser head, Sulzer metco twin powder feeder, IPG ytterbium fiber laser

Microstructure_Text.original: FCC + B2 dual phase structure. Small, irregular B2 precipitates (~0.42 μm) with volume fraction 0.5%, randomly dispersed within FCC matrix at cellular boundaries. B2 phase enriched in Ti and Al due to larger atomic radii and negative segregation enthalpy. Irregularly shaped precipitates and aluminum oxide nanoparticles also present. FCC phase nucleates and solidifies prior to B2 phase during LAAM processing. High processing temperature combined with ultra-rapid cooling (~10^6 °C/s) leads to thermal stress concentrated in B2 phase. Dislocations accumulate around B2 precipitates.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC + B2 dual phase; B2 precipitates ~0.42 μm, 0.5 vol%; B2 rich in Ti/Al at cellular boundaries; dislocation accumulation at B2 phase

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; strain rate 0.6 mm/min
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: 25 mm × 6 mm × 4 mm coupon, wire cut EDM
Properties_Info.Note: Room temperature tensile test; precipitation strengthening contributes 302.57 MPa; dislocation-governed deformation mechanism

Properties_Info.Note: At strain of 3.5%; two-stage decrease in work-hardening rate (Stage I and Stage III only); dislocation-governed deformation; B2 phase hinders dislocation motion causing high initial work-hardening rate

Process_Text.original: Laser-aided additive manufacturing (LAAM) using Kuka robotic system, Precitec laser head, Sulzer metco twin powder feeder, and IPG ytterbium fiber laser. 50% CoCrNi powder + 50% CoCrNiAl0.6TiFe powder feed. Parameters: 1.3 mm beam diameter, 570 W laser power, 5.4 g/min total powder feed rate, 1200 mm/min scan velocity, ~0.5 mm layer thickness, 90° rotation between adjacent layers, 50% overlap rate, argon shielding (purity ≥99.999%).
Process_Text.simplified: LAAM from 50% FCC CoCrNi + 50% BCC CoCrNiAl0.6TiFe powder; 570 W, 1200 mm/min, 5.4 g/min powder feed; prominent B2 phase precipitation

Process_Info.Equipment: Kuka robotic system, Precitec laser head, Sulzer metco twin powder feeder, IPG ytterbium fiber laser

Microstructure_Text.original: FCC + B2 dual phase structure. B2 phase grows to ~2 μm and surrounds the FCC matrix with volume fraction 5.7%. B2 phase enriched in Ti and Al due to larger atomic radii and negative segregation enthalpy, encircling FCC cellular structures. Enhanced segregation of Ti and Al at dislocation cell boundaries. B2 phase depleted in Co, Cr, Fe; Fe tends to gather in FCC phase (B2 precipitates from mixed liquid alloy rather than remnant BCC powder). More obvious cellular structures compared to x=0.4. High-density dislocations on FCC matrix side. Al oxide nanoparticles distributed within cellular structures.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC + B2 dual phase; B2 precipitates ~2 μm, 5.7 vol%; B2 encircles FCC matrix at cellular boundaries; B2 rich in Ti/Al, depleted in Fe; high dislocation density on FCC side

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; strain rate 0.6 mm/min
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: 25 mm × 6 mm × 4 mm coupon, wire cut EDM
Properties_Info.Note: Room temperature tensile test; highest strength; precipitation strengthening contributes 371.81 MPa; fractured after Stage I due to abundant B2 phase

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; strain rate 0.6 mm/min
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: 25 mm × 6 mm × 4 mm coupon, wire cut EDM

Properties_Info.Note: Elongation at failure; lowest ductility; B2 phase at boundaries impedes plastic deformation

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 3.5% strain
Properties_Info.Note: At strain of 3.5%; highest work-hardening rate; fractured after Stage I only due to lack of ductility; B2 phase causes sharp drop in rate as strain increases
